identity.name: School
identity.definition: A School is a physical or virtual educational site within a district where students are enrolled, teachers are assigned, and Renaissance products are provisioned and configured — serving as the mid-level organizational container that determines product access, data scope, and administrative authority.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How a School appears in grids and dashboards

attributes.name: Student Count
attributes.description: Total number of active students enrolled at the school.

attributes.name: Teacher Count
attributes.description: Total number of teachers assigned to the school.

attributes.name: School Name
attributes.description: Primary identifier. The official name of the school.

attributes.name: District
attributes.description: The district this school belongs to.

attributes.name: Grade Span
attributes.description: Range of grade levels served (e.g. K-5, 6-8, 9-12).

attributes.name: Student Count
attributes.description: Total number of active students enrolled at the school.

attributes.name: Teacher Count
attributes.description: Total number of teachers assigned to the school.

attributes.description: State-assigned school identifier for reporting.

stories.title: Manage School Users
stories.benefit: teachers and students are properly rostered, have correct product access, and appear in the right reports.
stories.whenClause: I add, remove, or reassign users at my school,
stories.thenClause: their product access, class assignments, and data scope update immediately to reflect the change.

stories.title: View School-Level Reports
stories.benefit: I can see aggregate performance data, assessment completion rates, and growth trends across all students at my school.
stories.whenClause: I navigate to my school's reports,
stories.thenClause: I see dashboards showing reading and math performance, assessment completion, and growth metrics for all enrolled students.

stories.title: Configure Product Settings
stories.benefit: product features are tailored to my school's instructional needs and policies.
stories.whenClause: I update product configuration for my school,
stories.thenClause: the settings apply to all teachers and students at this school and take effect for new sessions.

businessRules.title: District Membership
businessRules.description: A School must belong to exactly one District. It cannot exist outside a District context.

businessRules.title: Licensing Inheritance
businessRules.description: Product licenses are provisioned at the School level. Teachers and students at the school inherit access to licensed products. A school cannot use a product not licensed at the district level.

businessRules.title: Grade Span Consistency
businessRules.description: Students enrolled at a school must fall within the school's declared grade span. Cross-grade enrollment requires admin override.

businessRules.title: Admin Authority
businessRules.description: School Admins have authority only within their assigned school(s). District Admins have authority across all schools in the district.

businessRules.title: Data Scope
businessRules.description: Reports and data views are scoped to the school. School Admins see only their school's data unless granted cross-school access by a District Admin.

businessRules.title: Deactivation Cascade
businessRules.description: When a school is deactivated, all students and teachers must be transferred to another school or deactivated. Classes at the school are archived. Historical data is retained.

businessRules.title: Roster Sync Priority
businessRules.description: SIS roster syncs are the authoritative source for school enrollment data. Manual overrides are permitted but flagged for reconciliation at next sync.

states.name: Active
states.description: School is operational with enrolled students and assigned teachers
states.triggers: Initial provisioning, reactivation by District Admin

states.name: Inactive
states.description: School is closed or suspended; no active enrollment permitted

states.name: Reactivated
states.description: Previously inactive school restored to operational status

relationships.mechanics: Students are enrolled at a School via SIS roster sync. Admins can manually transfer students between schools.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many. A School contains many Students; a Student belongs to one School at a time. Typical: 200-2000 students per school.
relationships.sorts: Students within a School: alphabetical by last name (default), by grade level, by teacher assignment.
relationships.filters: By grade level, by enrollment status, by class, by teacher, by product usage.
relationships.dependencies: If a School is deactivated, all students must be transferred or deactivated. Student data scoped to the school remains for historical reporting.

relationships.mechanics: Teachers are assigned to a School via SIS roster sync or admin action. A teacher may be assigned to multiple schools.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many. A School has many Teachers; a Teacher is assigned to one or more Schools. Typical: 15-150 teachers per school.
relationships.sorts: Teachers within a School: alphabetical by last name (default), by grade/subject, by class count.
relationships.filters: By grade level taught, by subject, by product usage, by class assignment.
relationships.dependencies: If a School is deactivated, teachers must be reassigned or deactivated. Class assignments at the school are archived.

relationships.mechanics: A School is provisioned within a District during initial setup or SIS sync. District Admins manage school creation and deactivation.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one. A School belongs to exactly one District; a District contains many Schools. Typical: 5-500 schools per district.
relationships.sorts: Schools within a District: alphabetical (default), by grade span, by student count, by geographic region.
relationships.filters: By grade span, by school type, by status (active/inactive), by product licenses, by region.
relationships.dependencies: A School cannot exist without a District. District-level license changes cascade to all schools within.

structure.evidence: A School has a rich set of attributes: name, grade span, address, NCES ID, state ID, product licenses, admin contacts, phone, time zone, school type, and status. You can design a detail page for a School.

instances.evidence: Lincoln Elementary (K-5, 450 students in Springfield), Washington Middle School (6-8, 800 students), Riverside High School (9-12, 1,200 students). Every district has multiple School instances.

purpose.evidence: Schools are the mid-level organizational container for all Renaissance products. District Admins provision schools, School Admins manage users and product configuration, Teachers are assigned to schools, and Students are enrolled at schools. All product data is scoped to a school.

sipValidation.verdict: Core system object. The mid-level organizational container for all Renaissance products.

synonyms.notes: Used in admin provisioning workflows and API references
Reflects physical location terminology from SIS vendors
Used when a single school spans multiple physical locations
Legacy term from platform data model; being deprecated in favor of School

shapeshifterMatrix.description: Schools in a district listed with enrollment, staffing, and licensing data. District admins compare schools and manage user access from this table.
The complete school record — enrollment, staffing, contact info, product configuration, and administrative tools. School and district admins manage everything about a school from this page.
A minimal row for selecting a school during setup or filtering. Shows name, grade span, and student count — just enough to choose the right one.
The school displayed as a banner identifying which school a report covers. Provides context for the data below — no interactive elements.
A summary card for each school on the district dashboard, showing enrollment and staffing at a glance. District admins scan these to spot schools that need attention.
Schools matching a search query with district affiliation and enrollment numbers. Used to find and navigate to a specific school.
